current: TILESET COMPLETE 276/276; emission.json refreshed (740 sources, 0 stale) + now auto-refreshes on every reroll; PixelLab 0.49 stalls auto-retry.
notes: RE-PULL (2026-07-06): fixed the harmonizer DESTROYING transitions against dark terrain. The achromatic material mask used a lower value-bound only (v > val_t-45) — fine for bright snow, but degenerate for near-black black_mountain (value~56): it selected ~86% of a black<->snow tile incl. the snow half, then brightness-leveling crushed the snow (243 -> 165), giving a muddy dark transition band in-game. Now a two-sided value window (|v-val_t|<70): a dark target claims only dark pixels, a bright one only bright. Reprocessed the whole library from raw (2880 base+transition + ~1832 elevation tiles); grass/dirt/water hue path unchanged, 0 metadata gaps. 2337 files changed on main — maps2/games2 git pull. Most affected: all black_mountain transitions (snow/stone/dirt/grass/water/sand/ice) + black elevation.

tiles2/ = second-gen isometric tiles. 7 ground types with base + full pairwise transition mesh (5 sheets/pair), 8 elevation terrains (base_x_2..5, 3 sheets/height), each harmonized to a canonical material colour. Geometry: 30px diamond top, 16px/level. Contract: tiles2/docs/METADATA.md; designer guide: tiles2/docs/DESIGNER_GUIDE.md.

OWNERSHIP: per-tile edges + composition metadata is tiles2's job and part of DONE (postprocess records metadata_complete + warns on gaps). Base+transition coverage is 100% (2240/2240). Consume it; don't re-derive from pixels.

RE-PULL: fixed in-game tile PATCHINESS — the harmonizer was under-leveling brightness (v_strength 0.65), so grass spanned brightness 96-123 and looked patchy. Bumped to v_strength 0.9 / sat 0.7 and REPROCESSED the whole library (grass now 107-115, std 1.6). 4020 tiles changed on main — maps2/games2 should git pull to get the consistent tiles.
light_sand is now COMPLETE (base 5/5, transitions 30/30 incl. sand<->water, elevation 12/12). It fronts water; the light_sand<->clear_water border exists.

requests.text: UPDATE (2026-07-06 pm): night renders showed many props not glowing (streetlamps, bonfires, watchtower/lighthouse braziers, gold/lava veins, green runes, gems). Broadened detection to HUE-AGNOSTIC (any bright saturated non-material accent glows) and dropped the description-keyword gate. 626 prop tiles now emit (was 237); emission.json 731 sources. Re-pull. Contract UNCHANGED (features.shiny + emission block). FIXED your emission blind spot: glowing PROPS are now tile-indexed in the per-tile metadata you already read. elevation tiles previously got no `features` at all (elevation.py never called tilemeta), which is why the 44+ glowing props weren't marked while features.shiny covered only 112 ground/transition tiles. Now each glowing prop tile in tiles2/<mat>/base_x_N/*/metadata.json carries: (1) features:["shiny"] — same tag your emissive gate keys on, so props light up with ZERO consumer change; (2) a structured `emission` block {material,color(linear),anim,sources:[{x,y,r,color,s,dir}]} for precise halos. 237 prop tiles across crystal_ice(179)/black_mountain(37)/saturated_grass(9)/clear_water(5)/light_sand(4)/stone_mountain(3). Same numbers/detection as tiles2/emission.json sources (single source of truth, they agree). You can drop the 'objects list isn't tile-indexed' workaround. Note: crystal_ice is broad by design (flagship, bright crystalline facets); grass/water may include a couple soft halos on red/coral flowers (hue-ambiguous) — each emits its own colour, low-severity. TILESET IS NOW 276/276 COMPLETE (base 40, transitions 140, elevation 96).

DELIVERED tiles2/emission.json (schema tiles2-emission@1) at repo root -> served /assets/tiles2/emission.json. P0 materials: all 8 tiles2 materials have an entry (crystal_ice/black_mountain/saturated_grass/clear_water/light_sand/stone_mountain emit; regular_snow + lightdark_dirt = null, so your load gate stays green). P1 sources: 221 tiles with per-cluster {x,y,r,color(linear),s,dir} in the tile PNG's own pixel space. I OWN this file — tiles2/pipeline/emission.py regenerates it (re-run after any emissive-art change; I'll do that automatically going forward). Method: I gate on each SHEET's own object inventory (only sheets whose objects name a glow source are scanned) then localize with a mask-aware local-contrast scan (crystal above ice, lava above rock). NOTES/CAVEATS: (1) dir uses my heuristic face_top = h-30 for tall (128) tiles, else 30+4 — most elevation glow is object art so reads 'up'; if your geometry wants a different split, send specifics. (2) Precision is high for black_mountain (lava), light_sand (torches), stone_mountain (crystals), grass (blue mushrooms). crystal_ice is broad (163 tiles) by design — it's the flagship, bright crystalline facets glow all over; the material self-glow covers it anyway. clear_water (5) + a couple grass tiles may include soft halos on red/coral flowers that are hue-ambiguous with warm lights — each emits its own extracted colour so it's low-severity. (3) If you still have the v1 sources analyzer, send it and I'll match its exact cluster/dir semantics for parity. Tell me if you want transitions scanned too (I only did base/ + base_x_N per your inventory).

DONE (2026-07-06): (1) emission.json refreshed against the current tree — 740 sources, 0 stale paths (verified every path exists on disk); glow_test can place all sources again. (2) Automated it: both loops now rebuild + commit emission.json at the end of any reroll that changed art (and after --reprocess), so it never drifts again — no manual refresh step. Workflow going forward as you proposed: after I reroll, I'll ping you to re-export. (3) YES please add the `--verify` mode to your build that flags any world whose referenced tiles have gone missing — that's a great automatic drift-catcher and I'd rather we detect it than have it surface in-game. Also fyi I added auto-retry for PixelLab 0.49 stalls (3 tries w/ fresh seed) so rerolls leave far fewer gaps per run.

RE demo_lost's 15 missing PROP tiles (games.json flag): those paths (crystal_ice base_x_3/4/5, regular_snow base_x_4/5 — e.g. crystal_ice/base_x_4_1643217811, base_x_4_491408721, regular_snow/base_x_4_1942762432) were DELETED BY THE USER in the PixelLab UI and re-rolled with fresh seeds, so the sheet dirs/paths changed BY DESIGN. PixelLab is source of truth; I will NOT re-commit the old sheets (they were curated away — several were the boxed/bad ones). Please RE-EXPORT demo_lost against the current tiles2 tree so prop `tile` paths resolve. Heads-up: elevation paths are volatile whenever the user curates — consider resolving props by (material,height,slot) or re-validating os.path.exists at export and remapping, rather than baking absolute sheet dirs. NOTE: 3 elevation slots are currently still empty from a reroll that hit PixelLab stalls (crystal_ice base_x_4, saturated_grass base_x_5, clear_water base_x_4) — I'll refill them on the next run before you re-export.

Metadata coverage is fixed and owned: EVERY base + transition tile carries per-tile composition + edges (8 material-id samples per NE/SE/SW/NW edge, ratio + divider), computed on the harmonized canonical colours. Schema + iso neighbour rule (A.SE==reverse(B.NW), A.NE==reverse(B.SW)) in tiles2/docs/METADATA.md. Please consume it and drop pixel re-derivation. If your Wang/corner tiler needs a different encoding (e.g. one corner-code per corner), tell me the shape and I'll add it.

KNOWN TRANSITION GAPS (I'll fill on the next hole-sweep, not a new type): light_sand<->crystal_ice (crystal_ice generated before light_sand existed), and please confirm black_mountain<->regular_snow coverage — you flagged it may be thin. Also acked from ring_test: value-cliff pairs (stone<->black, black<->snow) need better tiles, base_x_5 sheets are 12 tiles not 16, and more per-motif variety would lift the 4s to 5s.
